title: Arts officers, producers and directors
description: Represent and promote artists, performers, and athletes in dealings with current or prospective employers. May handle contract negotiation and other business matters for clients. Prepare cost estimates for product manufacturing, construction projects, or services to aid management in bidding on or determining price of product or service. May specialize according to particular service performed or type of product manufactured. Analyze and coordinate the schedule, timeline, procurement, staffing, and budget of a product or service on a per project basis. Lead and guide the work of technical staff. May serve as a point of contact for the client or customer. (Based on 37 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Compose songs or create vocal arrangements, and write music for commercial mediums, including advertising jingles or film soundtracks.
Practice performances, individually or with other musicians, to master pieces of music or maintain and improve skills.
Transpose music from one voice or instrument to another, and transpose music to alternate keys or fit individual styles.
Rewrite original musical scores in different styles by changing rhythms, harmonies, or tempos, and arrange music composed by others.
Study films or scripts to determine how musical scores can create desired effects or moods, and confer with producers and directors on music placement.
Transcribe musical compositions to adapt them for a particular group or style, and create original musical forms within circumscribed styles.
Collaborate with colleagues to complete final scores and copy parts from scores for individual performers.
Perform before live audiences in concerts, educational presentations, and other gatherings, and sing a cappella or with accompaniment.
Interpret or modify music, applying knowledge of harmony, melody, and rhythm to maintain audience interest.
Meet with soloists and concertmasters to discuss and prepare for performances and determine voices and instruments needed for compositions.
Use gestures to shape the music during performances, and direct groups at rehearsals and recordings to achieve desired effects.
Study scores to learn music in detail and develop interpretations while using elements of music theory to create structures.
Select music to perform considering ensemble size, abilities, and availability of scores to ensure musical variety.
Conduct auditions and select performers for musical presentations, and plan and schedule rehearsals and performances.
Write musical scores for orchestras, bands, or vocalists, and position members within groups to obtain balance among sections.
Perform administrative tasks including applying for grants, developing budgets, and negotiating contracts.
Coordinate and organize tours or hire companies to arrange concert dates, venues, and accommodations.
Maintain programming logs as required by station management, and monitor and log transmitter readings.
Record, mix, and edit voices, music, and sound effects for performances and recorded events using sound mixing boards.
Prepare for recording sessions by selecting and setting up microphones, and regulate volume level and sound quality during sessions.
Diagnose and resolve media system problems and ensure repairs are made to audio and video equipment.
Coordinate the activities of writers, directors, managers, and other personnel throughout the production process.
Review performer information, such as photos, resumes, and videos, to decide whom to audition for parts.
Confer with technical directors, managers, crew members, and writers to discuss details of production, including script, music, and costumes.
Supervise and coordinate the work of camera, lighting, design, and sound crew members during production.
Study and research scripts to determine direction and communicate necessary approach, characterization, and movement to actors.
Direct live broadcasts, films, and recordings for public entertainment or education, ensuring production objectives are met.
Select performers for roles or submit lists of suitable performers to producers for final selection.
Audition and interview performers to match their attributes to specific roles, enhancing available acting talent.
Negotiate contract agreements with performers or between performers and production companies, acting as a liaison during this process.
Compile scripts, program notes, and other materials related to productions while conducting meetings to discuss progress.
Prepare actors for auditions by providing scripts and relevant information about roles and casting requirements.
Manage activities such as budgeting, scheduling, planning, and marketing for the production, ensuring adherence to standards.
Review assembled films or edited videotapes to determine if corrections are necessary before they are finalized for broadcasting.
Train workers in the use of equipment, such as cameras and lights, to ensure a smooth production process.
Supervise and assign duties to workers engaged in technical control and production of programming, ensuring quality and efficiency.
Conduct interviews for broadcasts and establish work schedules to assign tasks to staff members efficiently.
Decorate and organize costumes for performances, ensuring they are in good condition and available for quick changes.
Review scripts or production information to determine the story's locale, period, and required costumes, facilitating accurate design execution.
Create written contracts for bookings, and prepare and maintain logs, records, or databases of client information and agreements.
Develop or implement risk-assessment models or methodologies to analyze potential impacts and opportunities.
Collaborate with stakeholders to plan, schedule, and coordinate project activities to meet deadlines and ensure adherence to quality standards.
Monitor the performance of project team members and provide feedback to improve outcomes and address challenges.
Prepare and submit budget estimates and progress reports to track project costs, resources, and technical issues.
Negotiate with stakeholders or suppliers to obtain necessary resources or materials for projects.
Communicate with project personnel to identify issues and ensure project objectives are met.
Attend company meetings to exchange project-related information and coordinate activities across departments.
Produce, distribute, and maintain project deliverables and documentation, ensuring clarity and compliance with standards.
Create project status presentations for clients or team members to convey progress and achievements.
Consult financial literature to ensure the use of the latest models or statistical techniques for project planning.
Assess the nature and level of physical security threats to inform project planning and resource allocation.
